corridors.note: Pilot corridor. Denver to Salt Lake City via the Colorado Rockies (Eisenhower Tunnel, Vail Pass, Glenwood Canyon), across the Utah San Rafael Swell, then I-15 north from Cove Fort. Entirely missing from the map today. Waypoints pin ORS onto I-70 so it does not shortcut to the faster I-80 route.

waypoints.note: Silverthorne -- I-70 exit 205, forces the mountain route not I-80
Glenwood Springs -- I-70 through Glenwood Canyon
Green River, Utah -- I-70 east of the San Rafael Swell
Salina, Utah -- I-70 west of the Swell
Cove Fort -- I-70 ends at I-15
Nephi -- I-15 north toward Salt Lake City

corridors.note: Montana I-15: Butte north through Helena and Great Falls to Shelby (US-2 junction near the Canada line). Uncovered; Great Falls and Helena are big missing freight cities. Sets up the US-2 Hi-Line tie-in.

waypoints.note: Helena -- I-15 through the state capital
Shelby -- I-15 at US-2 near the Canada line

corridors.note: The Hi-Line: US-2 across the northern tier of Montana and North Dakota, Shelby to Grand Forks. No interstate shadows it (I-94 is far south), so it passes the shadow rule. Loaded with uncovered freight cities -- Williston (Bakken oil), Minot, Havre, Glasgow.
I-90 Billings southeast through the Wyoming Powder River Basin (Sheridan, Gillette) to Rapid City. Uncovered coal-and-oil freight country; both endpoints are existing nodes.
US-2 Hi-Line eastern half: Grand Forks across northern Minnesota to Duluth, completing the corridor into the existing Duluth hub.

waypoints.note: Grand Forks -- US-2 east start (existing node)

corridors.note: US-2 western half: Shelby over Marias Pass through NW Montana (Kalispell) and the Idaho panhandle (Sandpoint) to Spokane. Uncovered; Kalispell is a real Flathead Valley freight hub.
